tranche: UP11-3 T1 live pod-based endpoint proof
classification: replay-later with profile change (not replay same profile)

cp1_profile_activation.dry_run_verdict: pass (parse + plan clean, follow-up path warning expected)

cp2_first_attempt_rest_schema_bug.symptom: POST /pods returned HTTP 400 with {"error":"Extra input keys provided in request body","problems":["key provided in request body which is not in input schema: 'dockerArgs'"]}
cp2_first_attempt_rest_schema_bug.root_cause: RunPod REST v1 /pods schema uses dockerStartCmd (list of strings) not dockerArgs (string); our T1 body builder emitted dockerArgs

runpod_container_timeline_z.14:45:04: Pull complete; Status: Downloaded newer image for vllm/vllm-openai:latest
runpod_container_timeline_z.14:52:15: stop container (operator-triggered via UI Delete + parallel REST DELETE)

vllm_progress_observed_before_crash: no /v1/models response; proxy returned HTTP 502 throughout; never 200
cp3_endpoint_proof.crash_classification: rapid container crash-restart loop AFTER image pull complete; five start attempts in ~7 minutes
likely_root_causes_ranked: KV-cache OOM on a single 80GB H100 for gpt-oss-120b at max-model-len=8192 (MoE activations + cache bookkeeping)
GptOssForCausalLM implementation gap in vLLM 0.20.0 (very new architecture may need a newer image tag or vllm-openai:latest pinned to a specific version)
mxfp4 quantization kernel path missing or misconfigured on H100 80GB
cp3_endpoint_proof.not_classifiable_from_data: RunPod REST v1 has no /pods/{id}/logs endpoint and the container's stderr beyond the manually-captured vLLM startup snippet was not persisted; so the exact crash reason cannot be pinned without either another run with log capture or the RunPod web console history

cp4_local_env_wiring.reason: endpoint proof failed; no valid URL to wire. Local .env RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL stays unset by design.

cp5_heavy_lane_health_proof.delta: none; usable flip did not occur because the upstream model would not serve

cp6_closure.exact_blocker: gpt-oss-120b on a single H100 80GB via stock vllm/vllm-openai:latest + --max-model-len 8192 crash-loops before /v1/models is reachable. Root cause is most likely KV-cache OOM or vLLM 0.20.0 gpt-oss support incomplete; without RunPod stderr capture the exact vLLM error line cannot be named from this run alone.
operator_followup_for_next_slice: Pick a smaller / better-supported model for the baseline proof (DO NOT keep gpt-oss-120b on 1x H100). Candidates in program shortlist: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B (fits 80GB comfortably, well-known vLLM support), or gpt-oss-20b if it exists, or a vLLM example model like meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct for a pure transport sanity check
OR keep gpt-oss-120b but move to 2x H100 or H200 141GB and capture stderr via the RunPod web console (REST has no logs endpoint)

mutations_performed: created pod 16mfbjql06k2xp via live RunPod REST (12 min lifetime)
deleted pod via HTTP 204 (operator UI Delete + parallel REST call; both resolved the same container)
code fix landed to amof main: d3cdea2 -> promoted as 1423172 (dockerStartCmd translation)

ui_operator_actions_observed.gc_dry_run_via_ui: expected to report pod 16mfbjql06k2xp as terminal_state:EXITED zombie (T4 guard) with actions=[report_only]; dry_run never deletes
ui_operator_actions_observed.delete_from_ui: POST/DELETE /api/v1/runpod/pods/16mfbjql06k2xp -> 204; container stopped 14:52:15Z on the RunPod side

next_slice_name: UP11-4 T1 retry with a known-working model
next_slice_scope_hint: Keep the exact same harness (profile YAML + driver + runbook + $4 cap + 45min kill)
Swap model to Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct on 1x H100 80GB (smaller, well-supported by vLLM 0.20.0, proven publicly)
Profile delta: image_name + gpu_type_ids unchanged; docker_args becomes '--model Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --max-model-len 16384'
Success criterion unchanged: /v1/models returns 200 with the model listed AND one /v1/chat/completions test succeeds
Only after that proves the transport, reconsider gpt-oss-120b (which needs 2x H100 or H200+) as a separate slice
